things.name: Loch Ness & Urquhart Castle
things.description: Iconic highland loch with the ruined medieval Urquhart Castle — the world's most famous monster-hunting spot

things.name: Culloden Battlefield (NTS)
things.description: Site of the last pitched battle on British soil (1746) where Bonnie Prince Charlie's Jacobite army was defeated — a moving and atmospheric visitor centre

things.name: Inverness Castle Viewpoint
things.description: 19th-century castle on a sandstone bluff above the River Ness with spectacular panoramic views over the city and highlands

things.name: Cawdor Castle
things.description: Romantic 14th-century castle of the Thanes of Cawdor — linked to Shakespeare's Macbeth — with stunning formal gardens

things.name: Ness Islands Walk
things.description: Peaceful wooded islands in the River Ness connected by Victorian suspension bridges — one of Inverness's most loved walks

things.name: Inverness Museum & Art Gallery
things.description: Free museum covering Highland history, Pictish stones and natural heritage in the heart of the city

things.name: Chanonry Point (Dolphin Watching)
things.description: The best land-based dolphin-watching spot in Britain — bottlenose dolphins regularly chase salmon near the lighthouse

things.name: Victorian Market
things.description: Charming covered shopping arcade built in 1890 with independent local traders, cafes and artisan shops

things.name: Dores Beach (Loch Ness)
things.description: Quiet shingle beach at the northern end of Loch Ness with stunning loch views and a famous pub — far from the tourist crowds

things.name: Clava Cairns (Bronze Age Tombs)
things.description: Exceptionally well-preserved Bronze Age cairns and standing stones near Culloden, dating back 4,000 years
